meta.name: FSS Fatigue Severity Scale
meta.description: The FSS is a 9-item scale measuring the impact of fatigue on daily functioning, widely used in clinical and research settings.
meta.author: Krupp et al. (1989)
meta.reference: Krupp LB, et al. The fatigue severity scale. Application to patients with multiple sclerosis and systemic lupus erythematosus. Archives of Neurology. 1989;46(10):1121-1123.
meta.instruction: Please rate your agreement with each statement based on the past week. 1 = Strongly Disagree, 7 = Strongly Agree.

questions.text: My motivation is lower when I am fatigued
Fatigue interferes with my physical functioning
Fatigue causes frequent problems for me
My fatigue prevents sustained physical functioning
Fatigue interferes with carrying out certain duties and responsibilities
Fatigue is among my three most disabling symptoms
Fatigue interferes with my work, family, or social life

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your fatigue level is within the normal range.
suggestions: Ensure adequate sleep and regular exercise

ranges.label: Mild Fatigue
ranges.description: You may have some fatigue issues, but they have a relatively small impact on your life.
suggestions: If fatigue persists, consider screening for physical conditions

ranges.label: Moderate Fatigue
ranges.description: Your fatigue is noticeably affecting your daily activities.
suggestions: Consider seeing a doctor to rule out underlying physical causes
Learn energy management techniques (e.g., activity-rest cycling)
Consider whether psychological factors (e.g., depression, anxiety) may be contributing

ranges.label: Severe Fatigue
ranges.description: Your fatigue is very severe and significantly affects daily functioning. A professional evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Seek a comprehensive medical evaluation as soon as possible
A multidisciplinary treatment approach may be needed
